creator: wxs@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Wed, Apr 08, 2009 at 09:26:17PM -0400, itsemu wrote:
> On Wed, Apr 8, 2009 at 7:22 PM, <wxs@freebsd.org> wrote:
> 
> > Synopsis: net-im/pork: unable to recieve ims
> >
> > State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> > State-Changed-By: wxs
> > State-Changed-When: Wed Apr 8 23:21:36 UTC 2009
> > State-Changed-Why:
> > Awaiting more information from the submitter.  I can not reproduce this.
> >
> > http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=133488
> >

> debug when an incoming message should appear
> 
>                [pork_aim.c:102:aim_debug] DEBUG: LIBFAIM DEBUG: lev: 0: 
> 0x49 
>                                                                              
> [pork_aim.c:102:aim_debug] DEBUG: LIBFAIM DEBUG: lev: 0: 0xdc 
>                                                                                                                                            
> [pork_aim.c:102:aim_debug] DEBUG: LIBFAIM DEBUG: lev: 0: 0x1c 
>                                [pork_aim.c:102:aim_debug] DEBUG: LIBFAIM 
> DEBUG: lev: 0: 0x15 
>                                                                                              
> [pork_aim.c:102:aim_debug] DEBUG: LIBFAIM DEBUG: lev: 0: 
> 
>                                                                                                                                                       
> [pork_aim.c:102:aim_debug] DEBUG: LIBFAIM DEBUG: lev: 0: incomingim_ch1: 
> unknown TLV 0x0013 (len 1)
>                          {2}
>       [pork_aim.c:102:aim_debug] DEBUG: LIBFAIM DEBUG: lev: 0: 
> incomingim_ch1: unknown TLV 0x0013 (len 1)
> 
>                                                                                                          
> [pork_aim.c:102:aim_debug] DEBUG: LIBFAIM DEBUG: lev: 0: incomingim_ch1: 
> unknown TLV 0x0013 (len 1)

It's a bug in libfaim where the type of incoming message is not known.
Are these all IMs or just those that come from a specific person/client?
I was able to send myself an IM from a pidgin client without any
trouble.

This is clearly a local issue so I will be closing this PR but will be
happy to help you diagnose this problem via private mail further if
necessary.

-- WXS
